wine_bars_late.name: Le Volpi e l'Uva
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Around 350 bottles on the shelf and 45 by-the-glass pours, almost all from small terroir-driven producers across Italy and France
wine_bars_late.tip: Stand at the marble counter and ask Riccardo or Ciro for an off-list small-producer Sangiovese; the staff have been doing this since 1992.
wine_bars_late.description: Compact Oltrarno wine bar opened in 1992. Curated small-producer list, French and Italian cheese plates, the unhurried pace older enoteche were built for.

wine_bars_late.name: Il Santino
wine_bars_late.address: Via di Santo Spirito 60R, 50125 Firenze, Italy
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Six reds and six whites by the glass at any one time, roughly 200 bottles on the list with a Tuscan small-producer focus
wine_bars_late.tip: Wines by the glass come with a complimentary snack; arrive early to claim one of the four small tables before the room spills into the street.
wine_bars_late.description: Opened in 2008 on Via Santo Spirito, exposed brick, four tables, iron stools and burrata-and-pecorino crostini matched to natural-leaning Tuscan reds.

wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Pitti Gola e Cantina
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Hand-selected small-production Tuscan and Italian bottlings, many available by the glass with cured meats and local cheese
wine_bars_late.tip: Aimed squarely at small Tuscan producers; ask the owners for a vertical of a single Brunello or Chianti Classico Riserva.
wine_bars_late.description: Wine bar across Piazza Pitti from Palazzo Pitti, focused on small handcrafted Tuscan producers. Dinner runs to 22:30, the bar past 23:00.

wine_bars_late.name: Coquinarius
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Around 200 labels with a stated minimum 30 percent natural wine selection, curated by a long-tenured Florentine sommelier
wine_bars_late.tip: The burrata ravioli in pistachio pesto is one of Florence's most-quoted dishes; pair with a Sangiovese-led IGT from a small producer.
wine_bars_late.description: Wine bar and restaurant on Via delle Oche since 1999, near the Duomo. Local-seasonal Tuscan menu and an at-least-30-percent natural-wine list.

wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Fuori Porta
wine_bars_late.address: Via del Monte alle Croci 10, 50125 Firenze, Italy
wine_bars_late.wine_program: More than 200 labels with strong representation of small producers; signature crostoni and Tuscan charcuterie plates
wine_bars_late.tip: Walk up here after the climb to Piazzale Michelangelo; the back terrace has the longest evening service in San Niccolo.
wine_bars_late.description: Founded in 1987 by Andrea Conti at the foot of the climb to Piazzale Michelangelo. Crostoni, charcuterie, more than 200 labels with continuous list rotation.

wine_bars_late.name: Casa del Vino
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Small-producer Tuscan, natural-leaning, by-the-glass pours rotating with the market traffic
wine_bars_late.tip: Standing-only at the marble counter; the tripe-salad crostino is the local order alongside a Sangiovese glass.
wine_bars_late.description: Historic stand-up wine bar near the San Lorenzo market, run by Gianni Migliorini. Daily small-producer pours and cured-meat crostini.

wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca La Fortezza di Montalcino
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Wide-ranging Brunello di Montalcino vertical with around five regular tasting flights and five top-shelf flights, plus cheese, salumi and pasta pairings
wine_bars_late.tip: Climb the fortress staircase before the tasting; the panoramic ramparts run around the medieval walls and overlook the Val d'Orcia.
wine_bars_late.description: Inside Montalcino's 14th-century fortress, founded 1980 by Mario Pianigiani and Marzio Giannelli. Five-glass Brunello flights, blind tastings and panoramic ramparts.

listening_bars.name: Move On Italian Pub and Record Store
listening_bars.address: Piazza di San Giovanni 1R, 50122 Firenze, Italy
listening_bars.wine_program: Italian craft beers, regional cured meats and pecorino plates plus a small but well-chosen Tuscan red and white list
listening_bars.music_format: Vinyl record store upstairs, in-house DJ sessions and listening nights
listening_bars.tip: Vinyl shopping while drinking; the front-row view of the Baptistery turns golden after sunset.
listening_bars.description: Front-row table service across from the Duomo and Baptistery, a record store upstairs and Italian craft beer and wine pours below. Closed Mondays.

listening_bars.name: Mad Souls and Spirits
listening_bars.wine_program: Cocktail-led, with a tight by-the-glass list of Tuscan small producers as supplement
listening_bars.music_format: Live DJs through the evening, themed listening nights on weekends
listening_bars.tip: The Soul Warmers section of the menu is the bar's signature; arrive early to claim a wooden table by the brick wall before the DJ starts.
listening_bars.description: San Frediano cocktail spot run by Neri Fantechi and Julian Biondi. Tiny brick-walled room turns club-like after midnight when the DJ takes over.

listening_bars.name: Tenax
listening_bars.wine_program: Cocktails and Italian craft beers; modest by-the-glass list
listening_bars.music_format: Friday and Saturday club nights with international techno and house DJs
listening_bars.tip: Plan a taxi back; Tenax sits near Florence airport and runs latest of any Tuscan club.
listening_bars.description: Florence's longest-running club, opened 1981 near the airport. International techno and house DJs on weekends, with a wine bar room for slower pours.

candle_lit.name: Rasputin
candle_lit.wine_program: Cocktail-led; a small reserve wine list with Tuscan and Champagne bottles
candle_lit.atmosphere: Candlelit Prohibition-era speakeasy with 45 seats, rich red walls, no phones and no flash photos
candle_lit.tip: No walk-ins; ring the doorbell only after a reservation made through the bar's site, otherwise expect to be turned away.
candle_lit.description: Florence's original speakeasy, hidden behind a faint blue light on Borgo Tegolaio. Forty-five seats, candlelight, no standing and a strict reservation policy.

candle_lit.name: Officina della Bistecca, Dario Cecchini
candle_lit.address: Via XX Luglio 11, 50022 Panzano in Chianti, Italy
candle_lit.wine_program: House Carne Diem wine plus Panzano-vintner bottles available to BYO or to buy from the shop downstairs
candle_lit.atmosphere: Long communal tables above Cecchini's butcher shop, music, candle holders on the tables, communal courses of bistecca
candle_lit.tip: One dinner seating only at 20:00 in Panzano; book months in advance through the official site.
candle_lit.description: Dario Cecchini's upstairs steak house in Panzano. Single seating, communal table, parade of beef courses paired with house red and Panzano bottles.

candle_lit.name: Caffe Rivoire
candle_lit.wine_program: Glass-pour list expanded since 2023 with Italian regional bottles paired to savoury plates
candle_lit.atmosphere: Belle-epoque terrace overlooking Palazzo Vecchio and the Loggia dei Lanzi, lamp-lit at night
candle_lit.tip: Daytime tourists clear after 21:00; come for a late Negroni and the view of the lit-up Loggia dei Lanzi.
candle_lit.description: Founded 1872 as chocolatier to the House of Savoy. Outdoor terrace gives the best dusk view on Piazza della Signoria, now hosting evening wine and savoury pairings.

candle_lit.name: Gucci Osteria da Massimo Bottura
candle_lit.wine_program: Bottura-curated list with strong Italian regional representation, including small-grower Piedmont and Tuscan reds
candle_lit.atmosphere: Intimate green-and-teal dining room and a candlelit terrace on Piazza della Signoria
candle_lit.tip: Reserve months ahead via OpenTable; the 19:00 seating gets the candles lit on the terrace as Palazzo Vecchio illuminates.
candle_lit.description: Massimo Bottura's Florence outpost inside the Gucci Garden, one Michelin star. Tortellini in parmesan cream, a candle-lit terrace and an Italian-leaning list.

lounges.name: Locale Firenze
lounges.wine_program: Italian fine-wine list curated alongside the bar program, including Tuscan and Piedmontese verticals
lounges.atmosphere: Renaissance interior of Concini Palace with frescoed ceilings, atrium courtyard and modern cocktail bar
lounges.tip: The Foglia signature cocktail uses gin, hemp, mint, basil and lemon; pair it with the small-plates menu before moving to a wine course at the back.
lounges.description: Cocktail bar inside the 13th-century Palazzo delle Seggiole. Ranked among the World's 50 Best Bars, signature drinks by Fabio Fanni and an Italian fine-wine list.

lounges.name: Atrium Bar, Four Seasons Hotel Firenze
lounges.wine_program: Champagne list including Krug Grande Cuvee, plus a curated Italian fine-wine selection
lounges.atmosphere: Ground-floor lounge in a 16th-century palazzo opening onto an 11-acre private garden
lounges.tip: Reserve Champagne Afternoon Tea (75 euros) earlier in the day and stay through dusk for cocktails in the garden.
lounges.description: Cocktail lounge in the Four Seasons Florence with a private 11-acre garden. Head mixologist Edoardo Sandri, Krug-Grande-Cuvee Champagne list, set afternoon tea service.

lounges.name: Manifattura
lounges.address: Piazza di San Pancrazio 1R, 50123 Firenze, Italy
lounges.wine_program: Italian-only spirits and aperitivo bottlings; small wine list of Tuscan and Piedmontese pours
lounges.atmosphere: 1950s retro Italian lounge with potted-herb walls, lacquered Tuscan wood tabletops and tied-suit service
lounges.tip: All-Italian rule means no London-style gin cocktails; ask the bar team for an amaro flight or a vermouth pairing instead.
lounges.description: All-Italian cocktail bar from 2017 on Piazza San Pancrazio, vintage Italian bitters, vermouth and amari plus seasonal Bellini-style prosecco drinks.

fortified_specialists.name: Procacci 1885
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Antinori bottles from across the family's Tuscany and Umbria estates, including Castello della Sala fortified-style passito and dessert pours
specialty_fortified: Castello della Sala Muffato della Sala
fortified_specialists.tip: Order the truffle-butter panini with a flute of Prosecco or a Muffato della Sala; the Art Nouveau walnut shelving has been on this corner since 1885.
fortified_specialists.description: Founded 1885 by Leopoldo Procacci and run by Marchesi Antinori since 1998. Truffle-butter tramezzini paired with Antinori sparkling and sweet pours on Via Tornabuoni.

fortified_specialists.name: Obsequium
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Around 1,000 Italian and Tuscan labels with strong Vin Santo and grappa selections; tastings paired with cured meats and pecorino
specialty_fortified: Vin Santo del Chianti Classico Riserva
Avignonesi Vin Santo Occhio di Pernice
fortified_specialists.tip: Ask the Capecchi family to taste a Vin Santo flight including the Avignonesi Occhio di Pernice, one of Tuscany's longest-aged Vin Santo bottlings.
fortified_specialists.description: Borgo San Jacopo wine shop and tasting room steps from Ponte Vecchio, 1,000-plus-bottle Italian list with one of Florence's deepest Vin Santo and grappa cellars.

late_tastings.name: Enoteca Alessi (Wine Bar e Spirits)
late_tastings.wine_program: Over 2,500 Italian labels with a dedicated Tuscan room; tasting bar serving wines by the glass with charcuterie and pecorino plates
late_tastings.format: Walk-in tasting bar, scheduled regular tastings by booking through the family's Facebook page
late_tastings.tip: The two Alessi addresses on Via delle Oche split shop and tasting bar; the 33r spirits-and-wine bar room runs latest.
late_tastings.description: Alessi family wine shop on Via delle Oche since 1952. The 33R room hosts the wine-and-spirits tasting bar with a Tuscan-focused room from a 2,500-label cellar.

late_tastings.name: Enoteca Italiana
late_tastings.address: Piazza Libertà 1, Fortezza Medicea, 53100 Siena, Italy
late_tastings.wine_program: National wine library covering every Italian region; rotating tasting selections and themed evenings
late_tastings.format: Evening tastings, themed flights and library bottles available by the glass inside the Medici fortress
late_tastings.tip: Tour the underground brick vaults below the bar where the wine library is stored; tasting flights are pre-bookable through the Enoteca team.
late_tastings.description: Italy's national wine library since 1960, inside the Fortezza Medicea in Siena. National-scope cellar with Tuscan focus and a panoramic walking-rampart bar.

late_tastings.name: SaporDivino at Grand Hotel Continental Siena
late_tastings.address: Via Banchi di Sopra 85, 53100 Siena, Italy
late_tastings.wine_program: Curated Tuscan flight programme paired with cured meats and Sienese cheeses, with thematic Brunello and Chianti Classico nights
late_tastings.format: Hotel wine cellar tastings, reservation only
late_tastings.tip: Stay for the late session and book the in-cellar dinner; the wine cellar is dug into the 15th-century palazzo basement.
late_tastings.description: Tasting cellar of the Grand Hotel Continental Siena on Via Banchi di Sopra. Reserved Tuscan flights, hosted Sienese cheese pairings inside a 15th-century palazzo vault.

late_tastings.name: Gastronomia Morbidi
late_tastings.address: Via Banchi di Sopra 75, 53100 Siena, Italy
late_tastings.wine_program: Tuscan-leaning by-the-glass programme, with Brunello, Chianti Classico Gran Selezione and Vernaccia di San Gimignano on rotation
late_tastings.format: Wine-bar counter inside a historic Siena gourmet grocer since 1925
late_tastings.tip: The basement is an underground wine-cellar tasting room; ask for a Brunello flight after dinner.
late_tastings.description: Sienese institution since 1925 with a basement wine cellar pouring Brunello, Chianti Classico and Vernaccia by the glass alongside truffle and pecorino plates.

sparkling_rooms.name: Procacci 1885 Bollicine Bar
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Antinori-house sparkling flights including Cuvee Royale (Conte della Vipera) and Antinori metodo classico from the Tuscany and Umbria estates
specialty_sparkling: Tenuta di Capezzana metodo classico Carmignano
sparkling_rooms.tip: Order a flute of metodo classico with the truffle tramezzino combo; the Antinori sparkling list is the bar's anchor offering.
sparkling_rooms.description: Procacci's bar program leans heavily on Antinori sparkling flights paired with truffled small bites. The shortest, oldest Champagne-style program in Florence.

sparkling_rooms.name: Atrium Bar Champagne Salon
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Champagne-led list including Krug Grande Cuvée, Bollinger, Ruinart and Italian metodo classico from Tuscany and Trentino
specialty_sparkling: Tenuta di Capezzana Carmignano metodo classico
sparkling_rooms.tip: Champagne Afternoon Tea is the daytime ritual at 75 euros; in the evening the same list is poured by the flute in the lounge.
sparkling_rooms.description: Champagne corner of the Four Seasons Atrium Bar, with a marquee Krug Grande Cuvee listing alongside Italian metodo classico bottles from Capezzana and Bellavista.

sparkling_rooms.name: Tenuta di Capezzana Villa Tasting
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Capezzana metodo classico from the Carmignano estate, paired with the estate's Chianti, Carmignano DOCG reds and Vin Santo
sparkling_rooms.tip: Combine the Capezzana Carmignano tasting with the Vin Santo riserva flight; both are estate signatures.
sparkling_rooms.description: Bonacossi family villa above Carmignano, the 1716 Cosimo III appellation. Estate-only metodo classico tasting paired with Carmignano DOCG reds and 10-year Vin Santo.
